description_long: This is an old, weathered gravestone made of stone, standing upright in a patch of earth. The front face is inscribed with text, though much of it is faded and difficult to read due to age and lichen growth. The top of the gravestone is arched, with a small decorative knob at the center. The back features a carved winged cherub face at the top, a set of scales, and a skull and crossbones motif, all of which are typical of 18th-century funerary art. The stone is mottled with patches of white and gray, indicating significant weathering and exposure to the elements. The base is partially embedded in the ground, surrounded by a small area of exposed soil.
description: A weathered, upright stone gravestone with faded inscriptions and carved funerary motifs, set in a patch of earth. The back features a winged cherub, scales, and a skull and crossbones.
description_view_0: The front view shows the arched gravestone with faded inscriptions and a weathered surface.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the thin profile of the gravestone and its embedded base in the soil.
description_view_2: The back view displays detailed carvings of a winged cherub, scales, and a skull and crossbones.
description_view_3: The opposite side view again highlights the stone's thinness and the rough, uneven base.
